text.title: Edge and CDN Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Edge and CDN Strategy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Cache keys MUST include the headers / query params that vary the response: Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, auth boundary, A/B test cohort. Excluded headers documented.
rule.rationale: Wrong cache key serves the wrong response to the wrong user (privacy leak + correctness bug).

rule.statement: Origin shielding MUST be enabled: requests from edges hit a regional shield first, which consolidates origin pulls. Single-shield bottleneck risk mitigated via multi-shield setup for high-volume tenants.
rule.rationale: Without shielding, every edge fetches from origin = origin overload during cache-miss storms.

rule.statement: Auth MUST be enforced at edge OR origin, not both. If at edge, edge passes verified identity to origin (signed JWT, mTLS). Origin trusts edge identity via shared secret / mTLS.
rule.rationale: Split auth doubles bug surface; one-place auth with handoff is cleaner.

rule.statement: DNS MUST have a configured edge-bypass record (e.g. origin.acme.com) reachable when edge is degraded. Runbook documents the bypass procedure.
rule.rationale: Edge platforms have outages; bypass DNS keeps service alive while edge recovers.

rule.statement: Per-request edge-compute cost MUST be budgeted (e.g. $0.0001 / request median, $0.001 / request 99p). Cost > budget triggers a review (cold-start growth, regex catastrophic, log volume).
rule.rationale: Edge bills surprise teams; per-request budget is the early-warning.

rule.statement: Cache hit ratio MUST be monitored per route. < 70% on cacheable routes triggers a cache-key review.
rule.rationale: Low hit ratio = CDN spending without offload benefit; cache-key misconfiguration is the usual cause.
